seoTitle: How to Export Airtable to Google Sheets Automatically
metaDescription: Sync Airtable records to Google Sheets automatically using an automation trigger, Google account connection, and mapped field columns.
intro: Rather than exporting a static CSV every time, Airtable's Automations can push records into Google Sheets automatically whenever they enter a view. This guide covers connecting your Google account, mapping fields to spreadsheet columns, and testing the live sync so your data stays current without manual work.

steps.heading: Open the Automations dashboard
steps.body: Go to the Automations tab in your Airtable base to start building the export workflow.

steps.heading: Create a new trigger
steps.body: Set up a "When a record enters a view" trigger to isolate the specific rows you want exported to Google Sheets.

steps.heading: Add the Google Sheets action
steps.body: Add a new action step and select the Google Sheets integration, then choose 'Create spreadsheet row' as the action type.

steps.heading: Connect and authorize your Google account
steps.body: Authenticate your Google account within Airtable and select the target spreadsheet you want records sent to.

steps.heading: Map fields to columns
steps.body: Match each Airtable field to the corresponding column in your Google Sheet so the data lands where you expect.

steps.heading: Test and activate
steps.body: Run a live test to verify the transfer works, then toggle the automation on to keep the sync running.

faq.question: Does this create a live sync or just a one-time export?
faq.answer: Once the automation is turned on, it runs continuously, adding a new spreadsheet row every time a record enters the specified view.

faq.question: Can I export historical records that already existed before setting this up?
faq.answer: You can run bulk historical exports by manually triggering the automation on existing records or re-entering them into the trigger view.

faq.question: Do I need a Google account to use this integration?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to authenticate a Google account within Airtable so it can write rows into your target spreadsheet.

faq.question: What happens if I edit a record after it's already been exported?
faq.answer: This automation is designed to create new rows, so edits to already-exported records won't automatically update the corresponding Google Sheets row unless you set up a separate update trigger.
